name: Graph Data UI
summary: Client-side data loading and state management for graph specification data. Loads graph data from the server when the graph page mounts and manages sync status checking.
goals: Load specification and architecture data when the graph page opens
Automatically check if data needs syncing based on git commit

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user navigates to the graph page for a project
acceptanceCriteria.then: graph data is fetched from the server and stored in the graph store

specDetail.description: The GraphPage component and graphStore manage loading graph specification and architecture data. On mount, the store fetches data from the API and checks if the data is stale based on the last sync commit. If auto-sync is needed, it triggers a sync operation automatically.
parameters: GraphPage loads data via graphStore.loadGraph on project path change
Auto-sync check runs after data load via checkAutoSync
graphStore manages specsData, architectData, syncStatus, and activeTab state
Tab switching between 6 views managed by GraphTabBar

constraints: No graph data shown without an active project
Auto-sync only triggers when git HEAD differs from lastSyncCommit
Data loading errors are silently caught and result in empty state

syncMeta.baseCommitMessage: refactor(specs): migrate spec tree to unified node folder structure
